description:
lib.rs lines 276-283: When the backend returns a byte slice that is not 256 bytes, try_sign returns Err wrapping SecretError::DecodeFailed.

DecodeFailed semantically means 'failed to parse/decode a value' — a caller-data or format error. A wrong-length response from the backend is a backend protocol violation, better represented by SecretError::Backend{} (or a new variant). Using DecodeFailed gives callers no way to distinguish:
- 'the backend returned garbled data' (backend bug / protocol violation)
- 'the bytes could not be parsed as a signature' (format mismatch)

This matters for error handling code that matches on SecretError variants. A caller checking for DecodeFailed expecting decode/parse errors would accidentally catch backend length violations and vice versa.

The fix is to use SecretError::Backend { backend: "signature-adapter", source: ... } for the length mismatch, consistent with how sign_sync reports backend-level failures (lines 68-71, 80-83, 88-90).
title: RsaPssSigner::try_sign uses DecodeFailed for wrong-length backend output
